findings.message: Command body uses Bash (shell code blocks) but does not declare allowed-tools in frontmatter; least-privilege toolset undeclared
Command body uses Bash (shell code blocks) but does not declare allowed-tools in frontmatter; least-privilege toolset undeclared
Vague quantifier 'some' in user-facing output string: 'Context is good but has some bloat' — unmeasured amount

Hooks registered across 10+ distinct event types (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, PostCompact, SessionStart, Stop, SessionEnd, StopFailure, UserPromptSubmit, CwdChanged); any defect in hook scripts will execute on a very high proportion of user interactions
subprocess.run(['start', str(path)], shell=True) on Windows branch of _open_in_browser(); pre-scan flagged as HIGH

Generated fleet dashboard HTML hard-embeds Google Fonts CDN links (fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com); browser makes third-party network requests whenever user views the dashboard, leaking activity metadata to Google
Vague quantifier 'Some' in behavioral instruction: 'Some optimizations have side effects' — does not specify which
Vague quantifier 'appropriate' in Core Rules: 'Use appropriate models (with fallbacks)' — the Model Selection table already specifies exact mappings; the prose rule adds no information
subprocess.run(['powershell', '-Command', ps_cmd]) where ps_cmd is a dynamically formatted string; pre-scan flagged as HIGH
